questions.question: What will happen when you insert null values into the 'salary' column of the employee table?
options: The column will be updated with the current date.
A new row will be added with null values.
The existing rows will be updated with null values.
No change will occur in the table
questions.explanation: When you insert null values into a column, the existing rows in that column will be updated with null values, and new rows with null values will be added.

questions.question: If you want to remove null values from the 'employee_id' column of the employee table, what SQL statement would you use?
options: UPDATE employee SET employee_id = NULL
DELETE FROM employee WHERE employee_id IS NOT NULL
UPDATE employee SET employee_id = ''
DELETE FROM employee WHERE employee_id IS NULL
questions.explanation: You can remove null values from a column by using the DELETE statement, specifying the condition 'employee_id IS NULL'.

questions.question: What will happen when you insert a null value into the 'department' column of the employee table?
options: The column will be updated with the current date.
A new row will be added with null values.
The existing rows will be updated with null values.
No change will occur in the table
questions.explanation: When you insert null values into a column, the existing rows in that column will be updated with null values, and new rows with null values will be added.

questions.question: If you want to check for null values in the 'name' column of the employee table, what SQL statement would you use?
options: SELECT * FROM employee WHERE name IS NOT NULL
SELECT * FROM employee WHERE name IS NULL
SELECT * FROM employee WHERE name = ''
SELECT * FROM employee WHERE name <> ''
questions.explanation: You can use the 'IS NOT NULL' or 'IS NULL' condition in the SELECT statement to check for null values in a column.

questions.question: What is the purpose of using the 'NOT NULL' constraint in the employee table?
options: To specify the data type of the column.
To make the column updateable.
To ensure that no null values are allowed in the column.
To make the column nullable.
To specify the size of the column.
questions.explanation: The 'NOT NULL' constraint ensures that no null values are allowed in a column, which helps to maintain data integrity in the table.
